_README: Machine feed of Grounded MCP Sweep harsh-critic recs (human-readable mirror: SWEEP_CRITIQUE_QUEUE.md). flywheel_orchestrator.py reads this and surfaces undispositioned entries in promotion_queue.md as an exploratory candidate class — so sweep findings flow through the SAME engine + disposition mechanism as the substrate miner. NOT recurrence-gated (a deliberate human critique is valid the first time it's raised). Dispose via promotion_dispositions.json using each entry's `key`.

critiques.title: No window.WHShell test seam
critiques.should_be: expose read-only window.WHShell={mode,identity,role} (sibling resume.html has WHResume)

critiques.title: Password-reveal eye buttons are tabindex=-1
critiques.should_be: make show-password toggles keyboard-reachable (WCAG 2.1.1) or use a labelled checkbox

critiques.title: Auth modal tabs lack ARIA tab pattern
critiques.should_be: role=tablist/tab/tabpanel + aria-selected on the Sign In / Sign Up tabs

critiques.title: Onboarding-ladder items 42px on mobile
critiques.should_be: extend the mobile min-height:44px rule to #oh-onboarding ladder items

critiques.title: First-run next-step points 3 different ways
critiques.should_be: align verdict + primary CTA + onboarding ladder on ONE activating first action for a zero-data novice

critiques.title: 57 visible CTAs on the landing at 390px
critiques.should_be: likely fine for a marketing landing (Jakob's law); logged for honesty, no change without a conversion goal

critiques.title: Hand-rolled modals lack the dialog a11y bar (SYSTEMIC)
critiques.should_be: a shared whModalA11y(modalEl) helper (role=dialog+aria-modal+ESC+focus-trap+restore) retrofit across all hand-rolled modals. NOTE: a forward-only gate ratchet (validate_modal_a11y.py, debt=13) now BLOCKS new non-a11y modals; this rec is the retrofit of existing debt.

critiques.title: Header tap size depends on per-instance min-h utility
critiques.should_be: bake 44px into a shared .btn-tap class so a dropped utility can't shrink a header action (the Voice-Journal failure mode)

critiques.title: style=min-height:unset defeats the 44px default
critiques.should_be: row icon buttons -> 44px or a >=44px row hitbox; lint inline min-height:unset on .btn-* (inline beats media-query, so it bites phones)

critiques.title: AI companion overlay controls 9-22px on EVERY page (SYSTEMIC)
critiques.should_be: the shared companion launcher/overlay (wh-ai-trigger 22px, wh-ai-mic/wh-ai-send 14px, wh-ai-close 9px in nav-hub/companion JS) ships sub-44px touch targets on every page -> bump to >=44px (mobile-maestro gloved-hand rule + Fitts). Surfaced measuring voice-journal/pm-scheduler but it is shell-wide, so fix once in the shared component.

critiques.title: Global header search bar 42px on mobile
critiques.should_be: #wh-hub-global-search renders 42px (2px under) on phones -> bump to >=44px (shell header; mobile-maestro)

critiques.title: Inline style=position:fixed;inset:0 modals are INVISIBLE to validate_modal_a11y.py
critiques.should_be: validate_modal_a11y.py keys on class='fixed inset-0', so modals built with inline style (pm-scheduler #pm-edit-modal/#completion-sheet/#add-task-sheet, dayplanner #modal .modal-overlay) evade the C7 ratchet entirely -> widen the validator to also match inline position:fixed;inset:0 overlays, THEN retrofit those modals with the whModalA11y helper (extends sweep:platform:modal-a11y-helper). pm-scheduler has ZERO keydown handlers (no ESC/trap on any of its 3 modals).

critiques.title: Hand-typed status-string literals drift from the canonical DB enum
critiques.should_be: the dayplanner overdue bug (filter checked item_status != 'closed' while the schedule_items enum is done/in_progress/pending -> counted DONE items as overdue, 6 vs 3) was invisible to every static gate and only showed when live count != DB. Centralize per-table status enums in a shared JS constant (or add a validator that greps filter string-literals against the actual DB enum) so a typo'd status can't silently miscount a KPI. FIXED on dayplanner this sweep; the systemic guard is the rec.

critiques.title: History filter-chips 36px on mobile (deliberate compact)
critiques.should_be: the .filter-chip @media<=480 min-height:36px is a deliberate density choice (like inventory's compact row icons). If these become a primary nav affordance, bump to 44px; as secondary entry-filters, 36px is acceptable -> logged for honesty, no change without a usability signal.

critiques.title: Status filter-chips 32px tall on mobile
critiques.should_be: All/Overdue/Due Soon/On Track chips are 32px (<44 WCAG 2.5.5). voice-journal left its filter-chips 'deliberately compact' at 36px — pick ONE platform rule for chips, don't let each page drift. See platform-wide interactive-min-height.

critiques.title: pm-edit-modal lacks the dialog-a11y bar
critiques.should_be: #pm-edit-modal has role=null/aria-modal=null (no ESC/focus-trap) — page-specific instance of systemic C7. Add role=dialog+aria-modal+ESC+focus-trap (shared modal helper).

critiques.title: .btn-icon (collapse/prev/next) 28-30px tap targets
critiques.should_be: The REFEREE pass fixed .btn-primary/.btn-ghost to 44px but .btn-icon (sidebar collapse, prev/next date) stayed 28-30px — primary date navigation is below the tap-target floor. min-height/width:44px.

critiques.title: DILO/WILO/MILO/YILO view tabs 31px tall
critiques.should_be: .view-tab is 94x31 (<44). The primary day/week/month/year switcher should be 44px. Same per-class-miss pattern as the chips.

critiques.title: Schedule modal lacks the dialog-a11y bar
critiques.should_be: #modal/#modal-box role=null/aria-modal=null — systemic C7 instance. Add role=dialog+aria-modal+ESC+focus-trap.

critiques.title: Shift-selector pills 37px tall
critiques.should_be: .shift-pill (06-14/14-22/22-06) is 140x37 (<44). REFEREE fixed .btn-* to 44 but the pill selector class was missed — same per-class pattern.

critiques.title: .back-btn 36x36 tap target
critiques.should_be: Back affordance is 36x36 (<44). min 44x44.

critiques.title: promote-dedupe toggle label 13.3px + 18px checkbox
critiques.should_be: Lone sub-spec control on an otherwise clean page (modals already role=dialog+aria-modal, buttons 44px). Checkbox 18px + 13.3px label — bump label >=14px and the hit-area to 44px. Low-risk (checkbox doesn't trigger iOS zoom).

critiques.title: nav-hub mode buttons (All/Field/Supervisor/Engineer) 83x26 on EVERY page
critiques.should_be: .wh-hub-mode-btn is 26px tall — it ships in nav-hub.js so it's sub-44 on every page at once. Fix once in the shell.

critiques.title: 44px tap-target fixed per-class reactively — secondary control classes keep missing it
critiques.should_be: HOLISTIC (Phase 4.7): every Wave-1 page's REFEREE fix bumped .btn-primary/.btn-ghost, but each page then shipped a DIFFERENT secondary interactive class below 44px — pm filter-chip(32), dayplanner btn-icon(28)/view-tab(31), shift-brain shift-pill(37)/back-btn(36), nav-hub mode-btn(26). The class list is open-ended, so reactive per-class fixes will always lag. Establish ONE platform rule: a base min-height:44px on all interactive roles (button/[role=button]/tab/chip/pill/icon-btn), or a shared .wh-tappable utility, enforced by validate_mobile extended to compute per-element box size (its current class-parse misses inline + novel classes — blind-spot #1/#2).

critiques.title: Home dashboard CLS ~0.08-0.12 (KPI tiles/alert pop in without reserved space)
critiques.should_be: ops-home KPI tiles (open-jobs/risks/pm-overdue/low-stock) + the signature-alert banner shift layout as async get_hive_dashboard data lands -> CLS measured 0.077-0.116 (>0.1 'good'). Reserve space: give the tile grid + alert slot a min-height / skeleton placeholder so the dashboard does not reflow on data arrival. Timing-sensitive on local Flask (varies run-to-run); confirm against a production-like load. Measured by ufai_battery CWV buffered layout-shift.

critiques.title: Data pages don't expose their Supabase client (no live-parity seam)
critiques.should_be: logbook/inventory/pm-scheduler create their client inside a closure (not window). index exposes window.db; resume exposes window.WHResume. Add a read-only window.WH<Page>={db,get} (or reuse the memoized window._whSupabaseClient) so a grounded sweep can read canonical parity in-browser. Today parity falls back to cross-surface text match (e.g. inventory '3 low-stock' == dashboard RPC) + the journey-hive-isolation spec for RLS.

critiques.title: Header logo link is sub-44px + missing aria-label on many pages (copy-pasted)
critiques.should_be: The header `` ships ~28-36px (no flex/min-size) and often no aria-label, copy-pasted across ~40 pages. FIXED inline on index/logbook/inventory (flex items-center justify-center min-h/w-[44px] + aria-label='WorkHive home'). Roll the same fix across the remaining pages (or factor the header into a shared partial).

critiques.title: Add-asset FAB is obscured by the global connection chip (52x52 -> 52x8 usable)
critiques.should_be: axe target-size: the .fab (bottom:calc(68px+safe-area), z-20) is partially covered by #wh-conn-chip/#wh-conn-label (the global online/offline chip), leaving only a 52x8px tappable sliver. Reposition one to clear the other (raise the FAB above the conn-chip, or move the conn-chip) — likely affects every page with a bottom-right FAB. Cross-element collision -> queued, not blind-repositioned.

critiques.title: pm-scheduler 'Overdue' KPI may not match dashboard pm_overdue_count (26)
critiques.should_be: INVESTIGATE: the home dashboard's canonical get_hive_dashboard.pm_overdue_count=26 (task/item-level). pm-scheduler is asset-centric (31 assets, per-asset 'N tasks overdue' badges) and its 'Overdue' KPI read ~2 in this sweep. Confirm whether the two measure the SAME thing (then it's a cross-surface parity DEFECT to fix) or different UNITS (overdue assets vs overdue tasks -> relabel so the numbers don't look contradictory). Page client not window-exposed -> needs the page's own data path to ground.

critiques.title: Async list/tile pop-in drives CLS >0.1 on data pages (dashboard + inventory)
critiques.should_be: CLS measured 0.08-0.13 on index home-dashboard (KPI tiles+alert) and inventory (27-row list) because content renders after the data fetch with no reserved space. Give list/tile containers a min-height / skeleton so the page doesn't reflow on data arrival. Generalizes sweep:index-dashboard:cls-tile-popin to the list pages. Timing-sensitive on local Flask -> confirm vs production-like load.

critiques.title: 4 agent memory buckets behind one Companion face (assistant, voice-action, asset-brain, voice-journal)
critiques.should_be: Memory is keyed per-agent (session_key includes :agent), so a fact told to one agent (e.g. assistant) does NOT surface when the worker chats with another (e.g. the voice-journal widget). This is intentional routing isolation, but to the user it is ONE face. Decide: accept (document the boundary) OR add a cross-agent shared-recall layer (episodic) so the Companion feels like one memory. Agent-as-a-Judge: grounded in the live bucketAgents observation, not a guess.

critiques.title: voice-journal persisted the turn but did not recall it this run
critiques.should_be: The gateway persists + injects the recall window, but free-tier 8B recall is probabilistic for this agent. If reliable recall matters here, pin a stronger model for the synthesis turn or add an explicit memory section the prompt MUST quote. Track recall pass-rate across runs before acting.

critiques.title: 85 visible CTAs on the page
critiques.should_be: Hick's law: high choice count slows decisions. Marketplace browse is arguably Jakob-acceptable (users expect many listings), but confirm the toolbar/filter density isn't competing with the listing grid for attention. Agent to judge.

critiques.title: "save to watchlist" reachable 13x on this page
critiques.should_be: Per-listing watchlist action x13 — legitimate per-row repetition (one per listing card). Likely NOT a defect; confirm it's a per-card action, not a global dup.

critiques.title: "view" reachable 13x on this page
critiques.should_be: Per-listing 'view' x13 — per-row action, likely legitimate. Confirm vs. a redundant global control.

critiques.title: seller avatar/name reachable 3x on this page
critiques.should_be: Same seller appears on 3 listing cards — legitimate (that seller has 3 listings). Not a defect.

critiques.title: "close" reachable 9x on this page
critiques.should_be: 9 close buttons = the 9 hidden sheets/modals each carry their own close. Legitimate (one per modal); confirm none are duplicated within a single open modal.

critiques.title: "Pending approval" means different things on asset-hub, inventory
critiques.should_be: Same LABEL "Pending approval" but different SUBJECTS (assets, parts) — same-named ≠ same-derivation. Do NOT consolidate; DISAMBIGUATE the labels (e.g. "Pending approval" → per-page "Pending approval (assets)"). Surfaced by the IA streamlining survey (Phase 2).

critiques.title: "risk / hot / critical" job served 4 ways across pages
critiques.should_be: 4 pages answer the "risk / hot / critical" job a different way: High-severity alerts (alert-hub); Anomaly signals (alert-hub); AMC parts at risk (alert-hub); Critical assets (asset-hub); Hot assets (predictive); Risk ranking table (predictive); Risk heatmap (predictive); Top risk this shift (shift-brain). Hick's Law — multiple entry points to one job slow the user. Likely canonical home → predictive, others deep-link. REVIEW: confirm one job (merge) vs legitimately distinct role/context views (e.g. PM-overdue ≠ project-overdue) before acting.

critiques.title: "due soon / upcoming" job served 3 ways across pages
critiques.should_be: 3 pages answer the "due soon / upcoming" job a different way: Tasks today (dayplanner); Tasks this week (dayplanner); Due this week (pm-scheduler); PMs due (shift-brain). Hick's Law — multiple entry points to one job slow the user. Likely canonical home → pm-scheduler, others deep-link. REVIEW: confirm one job (merge) vs legitimately distinct role/context views (e.g. PM-overdue ≠ project-overdue) before acting.

critiques.title: "healthy / on-track" job served 3 ways across pages
critiques.should_be: 3 pages answer the "healthy / on-track" job a different way: On track (pm-scheduler); Healthy assets (predictive); On target workers (skillmatrix). Hick's Law — multiple entry points to one job slow the user. Likely canonical home → — (human picks), others deep-link. REVIEW: confirm one job (merge) vs legitimately distinct role/context views (e.g. PM-overdue ≠ project-overdue) before acting.

critiques.title: "late / overdue" job served 3 ways across pages
critiques.should_be: 3 pages answer the "late / overdue" job a different way: Overdue tasks (dayplanner); Overdue PMs (pm-scheduler); Past end date (project-manager). Hick's Law — multiple entry points to one job slow the user. Likely canonical home → pm-scheduler, others deep-link. REVIEW: confirm one job (merge) vs legitimately distinct role/context views (e.g. PM-overdue ≠ project-overdue) before acting.

critiques.title: "asset-hub" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "asset-hub" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (index, predictive). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: "integrations" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "integrations" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (ph-intelligence, plant-connections). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: "inventory" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "inventory" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (hive, index). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: "ph-intelligence" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "ph-intelligence" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (analytics, integrations). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: "pm-scheduler" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "pm-scheduler" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (hive, index). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: "voice-journal" linked from 2 page bodies beyond the nav
critiques.should_be: Deep page "voice-journal" reached from 2 page bodies BEYOND the global nav (index, logbook). Hick's Law — confirm each extra path earns its place; otherwise rely on the nav + one contextual link.

critiques.title: Page .fab occluded by the global Online connectivity chip (axe target-size, serious)
critiques.should_be: CSS-390 mobile: the page .fab (z:20, bottom:68px, bottom-right) is sandwiched by GLOBAL shell elements — #wh-conn-chip (persistent "Online" status, z:9998) covers its top + #wh-hub covers its bottom — leaving only a 52x8px clickable strip -> axe target-size SERIOUS (partially obscured; safe clickable diameter 0px). Cross-cutting: affects EVERY page with a bottom-right FAB (inventory/asset-hub/etc). Not a one-page tweak: define a stacking/offset CONTRACT for the shell bottom-right cluster (conn-chip + hub) so page FABs clear it — e.g. expose a --wh-shell-bottom-inset CSS var the .fab bottom respects, OR move the FAB bottom-left on mobile, OR have the shell reserve its zone. Designer/Frontend decision. Pinned cause: persistent Online chip at z:9998 over FAB at z:20.

critiques.title: Remove vestigial Stripe/payments UI — platform is FREE (no Stripe)
critiques.should_be: WorkHive is a FREE platform, not using Stripe. marketplace.html ships a vestigial monetization path: the "Connect Stripe to receive payments" button -> handleSellerOnboard() -> POST marketplace-connect-onboard (Stripe Connect onboarding). The fn is closure-scoped so the button currently no-ops (harmless). REMOVE or hide the whole Stripe/payments path (the button + handleSellerOnboard + any seller-payout/Connect UI) rather than wiring it up. (The battery flagged it as onclick->undefined-fn; the correct product fix is removal, not activation. A prior "fix" that exposed the fn on window was REVERTED.) Human disposes which UI to cut. See memory project-free-platform-no-stripe.

critiques.title: Same 'Overdue' label counts DIFFERENT units across pages (assets vs tasks)
critiques.flag: JOURNEY altitude live-drift (journey_battery.js, supervisor): pm-scheduler:overdue = 2 (per-ASSET, v_pm_compliance_truth) vs dayplanner:overdue_count = 3 (per-SCOPE-ITEM, sub='3 items past their scheduled date', data-rag-label='Overdue tasks'). Both numerically CORRECT for their own derivation (an asset can hold multiple overdue tasks: 2 assets / 3 tasks). NOT a stale-value/math bug. The VISIBLE .sc-label renders bare 'Overdue' on BOTH, so a field worker sees 2 vs 3 under one word and is confused = the Phase-3 'ambiguity of source' finding, now executable.
critiques.should_be: RELABEL the visible .sc-label to differentiate the unit, e.g. pm-scheduler 'Overdue assets' (or 'Assets w/ overdue PM') + dayplanner 'Overdue tasks' (canonical data-rag-label ALREADY says 'Overdue tasks' — render it). DIFFERENTIATE, do NOT consolidate (two legitimate metrics) and do NOT 'fix the math'. Corroborates sweep:ia:theme:late-overdue + sweep:ia:theme:due-soon-upcoming. Human disposes wording.

critiques.title: Unify pending approval counts
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · CONSOLIDATE · conf=high] units=['asset-hub:pending_approval', 'inventory:pending_approval'] · CONFUSION: Technicians see two separate 'Pending approval' numbers and wonder if they refer to the same set of items.
critiques.should_be: Create a single approvals dashboard; surface the combined count and provide filters for assets vs inventory. (canonical_home=asset-hub.html). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Distinguish PM due vs task due
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · RELABEL · conf=high] units=['pm-scheduler:due_soon', 'dayplanner:week_count'] · CONFUSION: Both tiles read 'Due this week', causing techs to mix up preventive maintenance jobs with daily tasks.
critiques.should_be: Rename pm-scheduler:due_soon to 'PMs due this week' and dayplanner:week_count to 'Tasks due this week'. (canonical_home=n/a). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Clarify shift risk vs system alerts
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · RELABEL · conf=medium] units=['shift-brain:top_risk_this_shift', 'alert-hub:high_severity_alerts'] · CONFUSION: Both tiles use risk language; users may think a shift‑risk entry is the same as a high‑severity system alert.
critiques.should_be: Rename shift-brain:top_risk_this_shift to 'Shift‑specific top risk' and keep alert-hub label as 'System high‑severity alerts'. (canonical_home=n/a). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Differentiate asset risk categories
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · RELABEL · conf=medium] units=['asset-hub:critical_assets', 'predictive:hot_assets'] · CONFUSION: Both show 'critical/hot' assets, leading supervisors to think they are the same list.
critiques.should_be: Rename asset-hub:critical_assets to 'AMC‑flagged critical assets' and predictive:hot_assets to 'Forecasted hot assets'. (canonical_home=n/a). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Eliminate marketplace UI state tile
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · REMOVE · conf=high] units=['marketplace:current_tab'] · CONFUSION: The 'Current tab' tile shows UI navigation state, which adds no operational value and confuses users about its purpose.
critiques.should_be: Delete this tile; UI should indicate active tab visually. (canonical_home=n/a). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Deduplicate marketplace listings view
critiques.flag: [SEMANTIC CRITIC · REMOVE · conf=high] units=['marketplace:listing_grid'] · CONFUSION: Both 'Listings in view' and 'Marketplace listing grid' display the same set of listings, causing double counting.
critiques.should_be: Keep one tile (e.g., 'Listings in view') and remove the duplicate grid tile. (canonical_home=n/a). Human disposes; if confidence<high VERIFY same-derivation first.

critiques.title: Form fields lack PROGRAMMATIC labels (visible label, no for/id) — platform-wide W-form
critiques.flag:
NEW battery method __UFAI.formAudit() (Gap 2) found 16 unlabeled fields on resume (now 9 after the basicsField template fix). The pattern is
— a VISIBLE label with NO for + input with NO id, so they are not programmatically associated; a screen reader reads each field as anonymous (or just the placeholder, which vanishes on focus). The .field/.wh-input/.wh-label pattern recurs across 15 HTML files (851 occurrences). Also caught: email rendered as type=text (wrong mobile keyboard / no validation) + missing autocomplete on identity fields.
critiques.should_be: W-form (mirror W1 tap-target = one systemic fix, not per-field): fix the SHARED form-field render templates to emit id+for (programmatic label) + the right input type derived from the field key (email->type=email, phone->tel, qty->number) + autocomplete on name/email/phone. Where fields are static HTML, a one-time linkFieldLabels() helper (generate id, set label[for]) covers them; for JS-rendered fields fix the template (as resume basicsField was). THEN add __UFAI.formAudit() to the L2 gate so it cannot regress. DOWNPAYMENT done: resume basicsField (7 fields: name/headline/email/phone/city/region/summary) now labeled+typed+verified. REMAINING: resume section editors (experience/education/skills ~9 fields) + the other ~14 pages.
